SQL is systematically structured from SELECT/filters → JOINs → GROUP BY/HAVING → subqueries/CTEs → window functions with solid practice, but Day 7 and other advanced interview-relevant SQL areas (set operations/UNION, conditional aggregation/CASE, performance/indexing basics) aren’t shown, so not a full 4/4. Python (Pandas/NumPy), visualization+statistics, and ML are only mentioned at the overview level but not actually covered in the provided detailed plan content, so they receive major deductions.

Difficulty Progression Reasonableness.2b_comment: Starting point is mostly appropriate: beginning with SQL fundamentals is reasonable for a Python-intermediate aiming for analyst interviews, though Day 1 is very beginner-oriented (heavy ‘intro’ resources) and could be shortened in favor of more practice/problem-solving. Weekly progression matches the recommended route (SQL -> Pandas/NumPy -> Viz/Stats -> ML/projects), but the plan’s stated emphasis on stats/A-B testing would warrant slightly more time earlier or a clearer bridge from Week 3 to Week 4. Within Week 1, daily difficulty ramps well: SELECT/filtering -> JOINs -> aggregation -> subqueries/CTEs -> window functions, with interview-style practice increasing. Comprehensive project at the end is mentioned in the overview, but the provided content does not actually specify an end-to-end capstone (dataset, deliverables, evaluation criteria, or timeline), so this earns a major deduction.

3. Resource Authenticity & Quality (20 p.3a_quality_platforms: github.com, leetcode.com, matplotlib.org, mode.com, numpy.org, pandas.pydata.org, scikit-learn.org, seaborn.pydata.org, sqlbolt.com, www.coursera.org, www.datacamp.com, www.hackerrank.com, www.kaggle.com, www.khanacademy.org, www.stratascratch.com, www.w3schools.com, www.youtube.com

No obvious fabricated domains/pages in the excerpt. Specificity: Generally good (named courses/videos + some durations and timestamps; specific SQLBolt lesson numbers; specific LeetCode problem IDs). Diversity: Solid mix of video + reading + interactive practice + official docs + interview-style problem sets; no textbook/ebook or full project dataset platform in this excerpt, so not maxed.

2) Time arrangement feasibility (1/3): The estimated time is marked daily, but several days are likely overloaded for 2–3 hours when you include context switching + account setup + reading + video + multiple platforms (SQLBolt/HackerRank/LeetCode) plus custom query writing. Day 6 window functions with multiple problems + challenges may also exceed 3 hours for an intermediate learner.
